a deer in the headlights.example_clue: To be frozen in fear is to be "caught like" this unfortunate creature, glare or no glare

a fox in the henhouse.example_clue: There's a small wild canine in this term for a treacherous infiltrator

a bird in the hand is worth two in the b.display: a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush
a bird in the hand is worth two in the b.example_clue: This 11-word phrase means it's better to have a sure thing than to risk it for double

a snake in the grass.example_clue: The Latin phrase latet anguis in herba is the source of this phrase for a pretended friend or lurking danger

a dog in the manger.example_clue: A person who has no use for something but selfishly prevents others from enjoying it is one of these

algae.example_clue: Rhodophyta, or red these organisms, act as calcified cement for coral reefs

circadian.example_clue: A tiny cluster of cells called the suprachiasmatic nucleus in the hypothalamus is integral to this day & night "rhythm"

muscle.example_clue: Our 3 types of this soft tissue are skeletal, cardiac & smooth

phylum.example_clue: In taxonomy this 6-letter classification comes between class & kingdom

synesthesia.example_clue: Associating letters & numbers with colors is an aspect of this phenomenon, from Greek words for "together" & "feeling"

ozone.example_clue: This layer in the stratosphere absorbs almost all of the Sun's dangerous ultraviolet light

a comet.example_clue: In 2025, we glimpsed the coma & center of 3I/ATLAS, an interstellar one of these objects rarely seen in our solar system

cartilage.example_clue: The tip of your schnozz & the boneless bodies of stingrays are both made of this tissue

lead & gold.example_clue: On the periodic table, these are the only 2 elements ending in "D" -- in 2025, the LHC was able to briefly turn one into the other

enzymes.example_clue: Protease is this type of substance that breaks down proteins into peptides & amino acids

artemis.example_clue: This space program's mission No. II made a lunar flyby in 2026; mission No. III hopes to put humans on the surface in 2027

pluto.example_clue: In July 2015 the New Horizons space probe flew by this celestial object & Charon, its largest moon

the asteroid belt.example_clue: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is on a voyage to visit 16 Psyche in this 2-word area between Mars & Jupiter

the (james) webb (space telescope).example_clue: A new take on the Hubble Ultra Deep Field was made by this telescope & released to the public in the summer of 2025

vanguard.example_clue: Launched in 1958, the oldest satellite orbiting the Earth has this name, a word meaning "forefront"

vertebrates.example_clue: Part of phylum Chordata, amphibians & mammals are termed these, due to the fact that they have backbones

the anther.example_clue: Often at the tips of a flower's filaments, it's the part of the stamen that contains pollen

the coriolis effect.example_clue: Ekman spirals form in the ocean because of the deflection of currents due to this effect named for a Frenchman

ash.example_clue: Volcanic dust consists of particles less than .0025 inches in diameter; volcanic this is bigger, less than .08 inches

nitrogen, phosphorus & potassium.example_clue: Bat guano is used as a fertilizer due to its richness of these 3 elements, numbers 7, 15 & 19 on the periodic table

a cell.example_clue: A mass of cytoplasm bound by a membrane, it's the smallest independently functioning unit of living matter

a wave.example_clue: An electromagnetic one of these can travel through a vacuum, but a mechanical one needs a medium to travel through

a volt.example_clue: This measure of electric potential is one of 3 units whose relationship is defined by Ohm's law

tilt.example_clue: "Obliquity of the ecliptic" is a fancy term for Earth's this, the angle of its rotational axis relative to its orbital plane

tin.example_clue: You might say we have this chemical element to thank for the Bronze Age, since the Bronze Agers couldn't make bronze without it

stem cells.example_clue: They're 4-letter unspecialized cells that can divide to produce specialized ones

heavy hydrogen.example_clue: Containing an extra neutron, deuterium is also called "heavy" this

anaerobic.example_clue: This adjective describes bacteria & protozoa able to live in the absence of oxygen

resistance.example_clue: Measured in ohms, it's the opposition to current flow in an electric circuit

lava.example_clue: From Hawaiian, aa is this stuff with a rough, broken surface

labyrinth.example_clue: The system of interconnecting canals & spaces of the inner ear has this name, like a maze of Greek mythology

plankton.example_clue: With a name from Greek for "drifting", these free-floating marine organisms produce about half the Earth's oxygen

antarctica.example_clue: National Geographic says it's the world's largest desert, its 5 million sq. miles getting only 3" of precipitation a year

-40.example_clue: At this wicked cold temperature, Celsius & Fahrenheit are identical

nanometers.example_clue: In 1801 Thomas Young showed light is a wave -- so it has a wavelength, which he quite accurately put at 22.7/1,000,000", about 576 of these

a vacuum.example_clue: Using a new air pump, in 1654 Otto von Guericke created one of these empty spaces so strong horses couldn't pull two bowls apart

symmetry.example_clue: In biology it's one side mirroring the other; in physics, the "time reversal" type means a law theoretically works backward & forward

kinetic energy.example_clue: In the equation KE = 1/2mv 2 , m is an object's mass, v is its velocity & KE is its this

ferromagnetism.example_clue: This type of attraction isn't limited to iron, from which its name is derived; it also applies to nickel & cobalt

gravity & buoyancy.example_clue: A body floating in water is at equilibrium between these 2 forces, one pulling down & one lifting up

natural selection.example_clue: Darwin: "I have called this principle, by which each slight variation, if useful, is preserved", this

quantum physics (quantum mechanics).example_clue: ( Andy Richter presents the clue. ) Who knows what really "controls the universe"? Even Einstein struggled with this mysterious subatomic science, writing that "an inner voice tells me it is not yet the real thing"

oppenheimer.example_clue: "We knew the world would not be the same" was his understated recollection of the explosion of the first atomic bomb

(carl) sagan.example_clue: This astronomer wrote, "We're made of star-stuff. We are a way for the cosmos to know itself"

newton.example_clue: With gravity, this great Brit wrote, "To explain all nature is too difficult a task for any one man or even for any one age"

a left arm.example_clue: Who knows what this part of Sandy Koufax could have done if ulnar collateral ligament reconstruction had been invented sooner

a lab assistant.example_clue: Francis Jehl, reminiscing here , had this job helping Thomas Edison: The 21st of October, 1879, the lamp had held out heroically

a local anesthestic.example_clue: Doc, do you need to put me under or will you just use one of these?

linear algebra.example_clue: For 60+ years Gilbert Strang taught this 2-word math subject at M.I.T., then retired to solve more unknowns

a sponge.example_clue: Barrel & fan are 2 types of these primitive marine organisms with porous skeletons attached to an underwater surface

singularity.example_clue: According to general relativity, it's a point of infinite density & infinitesimal volume where space & time are distorted

strontium.example_clue: The 90 isotope of this element is a dangerous component of nuclear fallout because it is easily absorbed by the body

sauropods.example_clue: The Apatosaurus is a member of this class of dinosaurs, plant-eaters of tremendous size with a stout body, thick legs & long tails

scatter(ing).example_clue: That gorgeous sunset is caused by this phenomenon: light rays collide with small particles & go hither & yon

doctor who.example_clue: A Time Lord from Gallifrey is the hero of this, TV's longest-running space opera, & has sometimes worn TV's longest scarf

queen.example_clue: Now a cult classic, the 1980 film "Flash Gordon" features a delightfully campy soundtrack by this band

amazing stories.example_clue: Called the father of space opera, E.E. "Doc" Smith's first novel, "The Skylark of Space", appeared in 1928 in this sci-fi magazine

serenity.example_clue: "Firefly" was canceled after just 11 episodes, but fans received a consolation prize in the form of this 2005 film sequel

starship troopers.example_clue: The only good bug is a dead bug in this 1997 satirical film based on a military space opera by Robert Heinlein

dna.example_clue: 2 years before Crick & Watson, Rosalind Franklin used X-ray diffraction to show it has a helical shape

flint.example_clue: Public health advocate Dr. Mona Hanna made headlines when she exposed the water crisis in this Michigan city

coral reefs.example_clue: Ruth Gates was known for her work on preserving these oceanic structures and combating the bleaching that harms them

(the) manhattan (project).example_clue: Chien-Shiung Wu , the first lady of physics, worked on this 1942-47 project and was the first female president of the American Physical Society

eniac.example_clue: During WWII Betty Holberton was an original programmer on this first general-purpose electronic digital computer

the scapula.example_clue: The bones of the shoulder include this one better known as the shoulder blade

mycology.example_clue: Thanks to his 1729 book full of sexy pictures of spores, lichens & fungi, Pier Antonio Micheli has been called the father of this

guanine.example_clue: DNA is composed of 4 nucleotide bases, adenine (A), cytosine (C), thymine (T) & this (G)

a vector (quantity).example_clue: The length of the line reflects magnitude, & the arrow shows direction, for this type of quantity like acceleration

chalk.example_clue: This white variety of limestone is made from shells of marine organisms; some classroom versions are made with gypsum

warthogs.example_clue: Males of this common pig species from Africa have pairs of bumps on their faces & 2 pairs of tusks

the ozone layer.example_clue: Good news! This layer in the stratosphere is healing, & that's not going to exacerbate global warming

the big crunch.example_clue: A candy bar created by Nestlé is in this nickname of the hypothetical model for the universe's end where all matter collapses on itself

transpiration.example_clue: This 13-letter word is the discharge of water into the atmosphere, mainly through the stomata of leaves

acid rain.example_clue: Put cut flowers into water mixed with different amounts of vinegar to mimic & observe the effects of this environmental hazard

wind.example_clue: Use paper cups, straws & a hole punch to make an anemometer for measuring the speed of this

a glow stick.example_clue: When these party favors are activated, that snap you hear is a small glass tube of hydrogen peroxide breaking open

non-newtonian fluids.example_clue: Mix water & cornstarch to make oobleck , one of these negatively-named types of fluid with both liquid & solid properties

trebuchet.example_clue: Use craft sticks & rubber bands to build a mini one of these catapults used in the middle ages & with a name from French for "to fall"

mendel.example_clue: In 1865 this monk presented the results of his breeding experiments with pea plants to the Natural Science Society in Brünn

battery.example_clue: In 1800, Alessandro Volta announced his invention, the voltaic pile , a predecessor to the modern this

chloroform.example_clue: CHCl 3 , also known as this, was administered as an anesthetic to Queen Victoria when she gave birth to Prince Leopold

(ada) lovelace.example_clue: A brilliant mathematician, she wrote the program for Charles Babbage's early computer prototype

faraday.example_clue: This Brit lent his name to a cage that shields what's inside from electromagnetic radiation

satellites.example_clue: About 2.5 times the parent body radius, the Roche limit defines how close natural these, like the Moon, can get before coming apart

venus.example_clue: In 1962 Mariner 2 passed within about 22,000 miles of this planet & confirmed its high surface temperature

a nebula.example_clue: The Helix & the Veil are 2 examples of these massive bodies of gas & dust, light years across, out in interstellar space

andromeda.example_clue: Scientists once thought the Milky Way was the whole universe -- then Edwin Hubble proved this nearby galaxy was far beyond ours

the schwarzschild radius.example_clue: When a star collapses to a mass equal to or less than this radius named for a German astronomer, it becomes a black hole

the mantle.example_clue: The thickest of the Earth's layers, it extends through about 1,800 miles of hot, viscous rock

antimatter.example_clue: In 1995 scientists at CERN manipulated positrons to orbit antiprotons, creating the first atoms of this once sci-fi stuff

red shift, blue shift.example_clue: 1 shift, 2 shift, these 2 terms for increase & decrease in light wavelengths from an object moving toward & away from you

stephen hawking.example_clue: The radiation named for this man is theoretically emitted by the space-time around a black hole

kelvin.example_clue: Water freezes at 273.15 this, & you don't need to add "degrees"

the elephant in the room.example_clue: The very obvious major problem wasn't even discussed, though we all knew it was this enormous herbivore amongst us

carbon dioxide.example_clue: Dry ice is what we commonly call the solid form of this gas

a can of worms.example_clue: What seemed simple has turned into a complex problem, like one of these containers of annelids

laughing gas.example_clue: Before it was widely used in medicine, nitrous oxide, aka this, was used for fun & recreation

a sitting duck.example_clue: At the comedy show I was seated in the front row, like this defenseless fowl

fool's gold.example_clue: Because of its similar appearance, iron pyrite is also called by this two-word name

the lion's share.example_clue: I love it when I get this largest portion of something, as would befit a savanna predator

the pleiades.example_clue: Despite its alternate name, the Seven Sisters, this cluster is actually made up of more than 1,000 stars

a one-trick pony.example_clue: I'm pretty sure he's only skilled in a single area as one of these equines

lockjaw.example_clue: Tetanus is also known as this because of what it does to muscles around the mouth
